title: No max length & control characters interpreted in `ps` command output
body:
For testing sig-proxy, I made some containers with a somewhat sizable bash script for their command, which became quite sizable after variable substitution (including many newlines). The entirety of this script is displayed in `podman ps` as the command, including newlines - see below:

`
6a7ef41a8dde   registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest   bash -c for NUM in 1
2
3
4
5
6
7
8
`
Omitted, for brevity
`
58
59
60
61
62
63
64; do trap "echo Received " ; done; echo READY; while :; do sleep 0.1; done;   2018-03-06 15:14:35 -0500 EST   Exited (1) 31 hours ago      zen_mestorf
`

This is obviously not at all readable.
We should escape control and whitespace characters in commands and ensure they do not overflow a single line in length.

comments.body:
Alright, Github formatting ate the newlines in my bug report. Reproducer command line: 
``sudo ./bin/podman run fedora bash -c 'for NUM in `seq 1 64`; do trap "echo Received $NUM" $NUM; done; echo READY; while :; do sleep 0.1; done;'``

Run this, then do a `podman ps -a` and you will see the command overflow a single line
